meta.title: SBD 1RM Calculator | Squat Bench Press Deadlift 1RM
meta.description: Calculate your Squat, Bench Press, and Deadlift 1RM. Check your SBD total, Wilks score, and strength level based on body weight.
meta.keywords: 1RM calculator, SBD calculator, powerlifting calculator, squat 1RM, bench press 1RM, deadlift 1RM, SBD total, Wilks score calculator, strength level, powerlifting total

form.rirQuestion: How many more reps could you do?

items.a: 1RM (One Rep Max) is the maximum weight you can lift for a single repetition. It is estimated using the Epley, Brzycki, and NSCA formulas based on the weight and reps you performed.

items.q: What SBD total is considered intermediate?
items.a: It varies by body weight, but generally a total of 4–5 times your body weight is considered intermediate. For example, at 80 kg body weight, an SBD total of 320–400 kg would be intermediate level.
The Wilks score is a coefficient used to fairly compare lifters of different body weights. A score above 300 is considered intermediate, and above 400 is advanced.
Levels are based on the ratio of your total to body weight: Beginner → Novice → Intermediate → Advanced → Elite, across 5 tiers.

items.q: How do I use the total-to-bodyweight ratio?
items.a: The ratio of your SBD total to body weight is an objective measure of relative strength. The higher the ratio, the greater your relative strength.

errors.invalidBodyWeight: Please enter a valid body weight.
errors.noLift: Please enter weight and reps for at least one lift.

footer.text: Based on Epley · Brzycki · NSCA formulas · Male Wilks coefficients

jsonLd.appDescription: Calculate your Squat, Bench Press, and Deadlift 1RM. Check your SBD total, Wilks score, and strength level.

faq.a: 1RM (One Rep Max) is the maximum weight you can lift for a single repetition. Since it's difficult to test directly, it is estimated using the Epley, Brzycki, and NSCA formulas based on the weight and reps you performed.

faq.q: What SBD total is considered intermediate?
faq.a: It varies by body weight, but generally a total of 4–5 times your body weight is considered intermediate. For example, at 80 kg body weight, an SBD total of 320–400 kg would be intermediate. Using the Wilks score provides a more objective assessment.

The Wilks score is a coefficient used to fairly compare lifters of different body weights. It adjusts your SBD total using the Wilks coefficient. A score above 300 is intermediate, and above 400 is advanced.

faq.q: How is the 1RM for Squat, Bench Press, and Deadlift calculated?
faq.a: Enter the weight and reps you performed, and the 1RM is predicted by averaging the Epley, Brzycki, and NSCA formulas. Selecting how many more reps you could have done improves accuracy.
Strength levels are based on the ratio of your SBD total to body weight: Beginner, Novice, Intermediate, Advanced, and Elite across 5 tiers.
